prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG planetary atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Warhammer 40,000 / Dark Imperium inspired grimdark far-future world map, dual-continent terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye imperial cartographic scan of a hostile hive world with two major landmasses separated by toxic seas and narrow straits.
Subject: two asymmetric continents divided by a diagonal S-shaped and L-bending toxic ocean corridor, not a simple vertical split. Include island-studded straits, inland toxic seas, jagged peninsulas, gulf systems, capes, broken industrial coasts, polluted river deltas, hive city mega-structures, underhive sprawl, promethium refineries, voidport platforms, manufactorum belts, trench-war wastelands, irradiated tundra, and gothic fortress-spires.
Style/medium: imperial sector star map fused with planetary atlas, gothic architecture aesthetic, mechanicus technical overlay, grimdark science-fantasy cartography, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape map, both continents fully visible, diagonal/S/L-shaped separation, generous toxic ocean border, subtle non-text cogitator grid and hazard sigils.
Lighting/mood: brutal grimdark far future, industrial hellscape, toxic ochre atmosphere, holy war and Inquisition dread, pyrrhic victory mood.
Color palette: blackened parchment, toxic ochre, rust red, ash grey, oil-slick teal seas, dirty brass, plasma glow accents.
Materials/textures: weathered vellum and dark metal frame, etched lines, auspex scan noise, engraved coastlines, gothic technical detail.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid mirrored symmetry and avoid a clean vertical division.
